name: Pineapple Upside-Down Cake III
recipeInstructions:
PREHEAT OVEN TO 375°F. Cream together granulated sugar and 1/2 cup butter at medium speed in a mixer bowl until smooth. Lower the speed; add vanilla, nutmeg, eggs, yolks, and milk. Mix until incorporated. Add baking powder and salt, then flour and cornmeal. Mix until fluffy. Set aside.
Combine remaining 1/4 cup butter, Madeira, and pineapple in a heavy, 10-inch oven-proof skillet over medium heat and cook for 20 minutes. The pineapple will release a lot of liquid. Remove the pineapple with a slotted spoon and reserve it. Continue cooking until the liquid is reduced nearly to a glaze. Add brown sugar and cook until the mixture becomes smooth, about 2 minutes.
Replace the pineapple slices and arrange them nicely in syrup. Pour the cake batter over the fruit. Bake on the middle rack for 20 to 25 minutes. When done, a wooden pick inserted into the center of the cake will come out clean. Place a large platter over the skillet and turn it upside down to remove the cake from the skillet. Let cool before serving.
